title: Week of April 6, 2026
summary: This week brought major platform stability improvements that make OpsPal more reliable for daily RevOps work. The platform now automatically cleans up outdated files and fixes missing dependencies during startup, preventing common session failures. Most importantly, OpsPal now prevents invisible field additions when Salesforce Dynamic Forms is active and automatically assigns record types to prevent configuration errors.

entries.headline: Platform automatically cleans up old files during updates
details: Removes outdated cache files older than 7 days
Cleans up abandoned session files older than 5 minutes
Warns when memory usage reports become too large

entries.headline: HubSpot integration loads faster and more reliably
Platform removes broken integrations automatically across all systems
details: Works on Windows, Linux, and Mac computers
Detects and removes any broken marketplace integrations
Cleans up all plugin types, not just specific ones

entries.headline: Broken integrations get cleaned up automatically when you start OpsPal
Salesforce integration starts up cleaner with better error handling
details: Removes outdated quality audit tools from testing workflows
Migrates older integration settings to new format
Adds proper cleanup for Salesforce quality and architecture auditing tools

entries.headline: Critical Salesforce configuration fixes prevent common errors
details: Prevents invisible field additions when Dynamic Forms is active on Salesforce pages
Automatically assigns record types to System Administrator profile to prevent reference errors
Converts blocking routing errors to warnings so sessions continue running

entries.headline: Platform automatically installs missing dependencies and shows clear error messages
details: Captures detailed installation progress and error information
Shows warning and manual fix commands when automatic installation fails
Runs background fixes for safe dependency issues during startup

entries.headline: Eliminated false missing package errors during startup
